title: optimize_functions_to_subcolumns leads to incorrect result for ALIAS columns
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
```
CREATE TABLE t
(
id String,
t Array(String),
s Array(String) ALIAS t
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
ORDER BY (id)
as select '1', ['t'];
SELECT count() FROM t WHERE not empty(s);
+-count()-+
1. |       0 | -- expected 1
+---------+
SELECT count() FROM t WHERE not empty(s) settings optimize_functions_to_subcolumns=0;
+-count()-+
1. |       1 | -- correct
+---------+
SELECT count() FROM t WHERE not empty(s) settings asterisk_include_alias_columns=1;
+-count()-+
1. |       0 |  -- expected 1
+---------+
SELECT * FROM t WHERE not empty(s) settings asterisk_include_alias_columns=1;
+-id-+-t-----+-s-----+
1. | 1  | ['t'] | ['t'] | -- correct
+----+-------+-------+
```
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/91805b9e-d99e-4714-815c-8475ddb9a86c
### Expected behavior
_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

ENGINE = MergeTree()
ORDER BY (id)

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt32, data Array(Int32), alias_data Array(Int32) ALIAS data) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT count() FROM test_table WHERE not empty(alias_data) SETTINGS optimize_functions_to_subcolumns=1;
SELECT count() FROM test_table WHERE not empty(alias_data) SETTINGS asterisk_include_alias_columns=1;
SELECT id, alias_data FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
